description: Polish the docs UI layout, alignment, and text spacing to production-grade visual quality. Fixes inconsistent heading rhythm, paragraph-to-block gaps, card alignment, tab border doubling, breadcrumb spacing, inline code line height, callout padding, and several other layout defects identified in a thorough audit.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Fix inconsistent heading vertical rhythm in MDX components
userStories.description: As a reader, I want heading spacing to follow a consistent descending scale so that section boundaries are visually clear and hierarchical.
acceptanceCriteria: h1 uses mt-12 mb-5
h2 uses mt-10 mb-4
h3 uses mt-8 mb-3 (unchanged)
h4 uses mt-6 mb-2
h5 uses mt-6 mb-2
h6 uses mt-4 mb-2 (unchanged)
Top margins are monotonically decreasing: h1 > h2 > h3 > h4 >= h5 > h6
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/mdx-components.tsx lines 84-112. The heading components are defined as inline arrow functions in the getMDXComponents return object. Current values: h1 mt-8 mb-4, h2 mt-12 mb-4, h3 mt-8 mb-3, h4/h5/h6 mt-4 mb-2. Change to: h1 mt-12 mb-5, h2 mt-10 mb-4, h3 mt-8 mb-3 (keep), h4 mt-6 mb-2, h5 mt-6 mb-2, h6 mt-4 mb-2 (keep). This creates a clean descending scale.

userStories.title: Reduce paragraph bottom margin for tighter text-to-block flow
userStories.description: As a reader, I want the gap between a paragraph and the following block element (code block, callout, table) to feel proportional to inter-paragraph spacing.
acceptanceCriteria: p element uses mb-4 instead of mb-5
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/mdx-components.tsx line 115. Change 'mb-5 font-sans text-base leading-relaxed' to 'mb-4 font-sans text-base leading-relaxed'. Single class change.

userStories.title: Add border-radius to homepage beta badge
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want all bordered elements on the homepage to have consistent rounded corners matching the RetroUI design system.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Now in beta' badge div includes the 'rounded' class
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/app/page.tsx line 48. The className is 'border-border bg-primary mb-6 inline-flex items-center gap-2 border-2 px-3 py-1.5 font-sans text-sm font-semibold'. Add 'rounded' to the className string. Every other bordered element in the RetroUI system uses the --radius token which maps to 0.5rem.

userStories.title: Make homepage feature cards stretch to equal height in grid rows
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want feature cards in the same row to always appear the same height regardless of content length.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature card div includes 'h-full' class
Cards in the same grid row render at equal height
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/app/page.tsx line 80. The card className is 'border-border bg-card flex flex-col gap-3 border-2 p-6 shadow-md transition-all hover:translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow'. Add 'h-full' to make each card stretch to its CSS Grid cell height. The grid parent already uses grid-cols-3 which handles row height equalization — the child just needs h-full to fill it.

userStories.title: Increase gap between step number box and title in 'How it works' section
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the numbered step boxes on the homepage to have sufficient breathing room before the step title.
acceptanceCriteria: Step number box div uses mb-4 instead of mb-3
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/app/page.tsx line 113. The step number box className contains 'mb-3'. Change to 'mb-4'. This gives the visually heavy 48px number box more breathing room before the h3 title below it.

userStories.title: Remove top border from tab content panel to eliminate double-border artifact
userStories.description: As a reader, I want tabs to have a clean visual connection between the trigger list and content panel without a double-border line.
acceptanceCriteria: RetroTab content panel uses 'border-x-2 border-b-2' or equivalent instead of 'border-2'
No visible double-border between tab triggers and tab content
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx line 113. Change 'border-border mt-0 w-full border-2 p-4 font-sans data-[state=inactive]:hidden' to 'border-border mt-0 w-full border-x-2 border-b-2 p-4 font-sans data-[state=inactive]:hidden'. This removes the top border from the content panel since the tab list's border-b-2 already provides the visual separator above.

acceptanceCriteria: Footer container uses pt-6 instead of pt-8

userStories.title: Reduce breadcrumb bottom margin from mb-4 to mb-2
userStories.description: As a reader, I want the breadcrumb to feel closely attached to the page title it labels rather than floating with excessive whitespace.
acceptanceCriteria: Breadcrumb nav uses mb-2 instead of mb-4
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/components/retro-page-breadcrumb.tsx line 19. Change 'mb-4' to 'mb-2' in the nav element's className. Single class change.

userStories.title: Reduce inline code vertical padding to prevent uneven line heights
userStories.description: As a reader, I want paragraphs containing inline code to have even line spacing without visible height shifts.
acceptanceCriteria: Inline code element uses py-px instead of py-0.5
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/mdx-components.tsx line 152. Change 'bg-muted border-border border-2 px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono text-sm font-semibold break-words' to 'bg-muted border-border border-2 px-1.5 py-px font-mono text-sm font-semibold break-words'. py-px = 1px vs py-0.5 = 2px. With border-2 (2px each side), total vertical intrusion goes from 8px to 6px — within leading-relaxed tolerance.

userStories.title: Fix list item internal paragraph spacing for rich content
userStories.description: As a reader, I want list items containing paragraphs and code blocks to have proportional internal spacing rather than the global paragraph margin.
acceptanceCriteria: Paragraph elements inside list items use tighter bottom margin than the global mb-4
li component wraps children or applies a CSS class that reduces nested p margin
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes: Edit docs/mdx-components.tsx. The li component at line 123-124 currently just passes through. Add a wrapper class that targets nested paragraphs. The cleanest approach: add a class to the li element like '[&>p]:mb-2' (Tailwind arbitrary variant). This uses Tailwind's child selector to reduce paragraph bottom margin within list items from mb-4 to mb-2 (0.5rem), creating proportional spacing. Change line 124 from 'font-sans text-base leading-relaxed' to 'font-sans text-base leading-relaxed [&>p]:mb-2'. This is a Tailwind-idiomatic solution — no CSS file changes needed.
